name: Camino del Cid: Molina de Aragón and Castilnuevo Loop
name_original: Castillo de Molina de Aragón por el Camino del Cid — circular por el Geoparque de Molina y el Alto Tajo
description: Jog the 7.9-mile Camino del Cid: Molina de Aragón and Castilnuevo Loop, a moderate route through historical towns and the Alto Tajo Natural
region_intro: Jogging routes around Molina de Aragón traverse a diverse landscape within the Molina-Alto Tajo Geopark and the Alto Tajo Natural Park. The region is characterized by extensive river gorges, canyons carved by rivers like the Tajo and Gallo, and vast open areas known as the Parameras of Maranchón and Sierra Molina. Runners can experience varied terrain, including the spectacular reddish canyon of Barranco de la Hoz, and explore routes that incorporate historical features such as the Castle of Molina de Aragón. Best jogging routes around Molina de Aragón The most popular jogging route is Camino del Cid: Molina de Aragón and Castilnuevo Loop, a 7.9 miles (12.7 km) trail that takes about 1 hour 25 minutes to complete, incorporating historical elements of the Camino del Cid. Another top favourite among local runners is El Rodenal Trail, a moderate 7.9 miles (12.7 km) path that winds through the natural landscapes of the Alto Tajo Natural Park. Local runners also love the Castle of Molina de Aragón – Molina de Aragón loop from Molina de Aragón, a 7.1 miles (11.5 km) trail leading through the historic area around the castle, often completed in about 1 hour 13 minutes. Jogging around Molina de Aragón is defined by river gorges, historic castles, and varied terrain within natural parks, offering options for different ability levels. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 37 runners have used komoot to explore Molina de Aragón's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on running routes around Molina de Aragón?
A: The running routes in Molina de Aragón traverse a diverse landscape within the &lt;strong&gt;Molina-Alto Tajo Geopark&lt;/strong&gt; and the &lt;strong&gt;Alto Tajo Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt;. You'll find extensive river gorges, impressive canyons carved by rivers like the Tajo and Gallo, and vast open areas known as the Parameras of Maranchón and Sierra Molina. Expect varied terrain, including the spectacular reddish canyon of Barranco de la Hoz, and paths through juniper, pine, oak, and holly groves.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in the Molina de Aragón area?
A: Yes, many of the trails in the region are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1031411653/camino-del-cid-molina-de-aragon-and-castilnuevo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino del Cid: Molina de Aragón and Castilnuevo Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 12.7 km circular route. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31433523" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Molina de Aragón – Molina de Aragón loop from Molina de Aragón&lt;/a&gt;, which is 11.5 km and takes you through the historic area around the castle.

Q: What historical landmarks can I see while jogging in Molina de Aragón?
A: Molina de Aragón is rich in history. Many running routes incorporate historical features. You can explore the area around the imposing &lt;strong&gt;Castle of Molina de Aragón&lt;/strong&gt;, one of the largest in the province, which offers panoramic views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31433523" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Molina de Aragón – Molina de Aragón loop from Molina de Aragón&lt;/a&gt; is a great way to see it. Other historical sights include the ancient walls and the prominent &lt;strong&gt;Tower of Aragón&lt;/strong&gt;, the charming medieval streets, the historic Jewish and Morería quarters, and the picturesque &lt;strong&gt;Roman Bridge&lt;/strong&gt; over the Gallo River.

Q: Are there any easy running routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: While all routes in this guide are rated as 'moderate', the region offers a wide network of signposted trails within the Geopark and Natural Park. For a less strenuous experience, consider shorter sections of the listed routes or explore the well-maintained paths within Molina de Aragón town itself. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31434236" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tower of Aragón – Castle of Molina de Aragón loop from Molina de Aragón&lt;/a&gt; is the shortest route in this guide at 5.9 km, making it a good option for those looking for a quicker run.

Q: What natural attractions can I discover along the running trails?
A: The region is home to stunning natural features. You can run through the &lt;strong&gt;Molina-Alto Tajo Geopark&lt;/strong&gt; and the &lt;strong&gt;Alto Tajo Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt;, known for their significant geological formations and river gorges. A standout is the &lt;strong&gt;Barranco de la Hoz&lt;/strong&gt;, a spectacular reddish canyon carved by the Gallo River, located just 15 kilometers from Molina de Aragón. You might also encounter diverse ecosystems with juniper, pine, oak, and holly groves, as well as several lagoons and even small waterfalls.

Q: Are there any routes that offer great viewpoints?
A: Absolutely. The elevated position of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5302621" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Molina de Aragón&lt;/a&gt; provides panoramic views of the surrounding area, and routes that pass by it, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20143284" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Molina de Aragón – Tower of Aragón loop from Molina de Aragón&lt;/a&gt;, offer excellent vistas. Additionally, the &lt;strong&gt;Barranco de la Hoz&lt;/strong&gt; area, particularly the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4322842" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Barranco de la Hoz&lt;/a&gt;, is famed for its breathtaking scenery and unique beauty.

Q: How long are the typical running routes in Molina de Aragón?
A: The running routes in this guide range from approximately 5.9 km to 12.7 km. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31434236" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tower of Aragón – Castle of Molina de Aragón loop from Molina de Aragón&lt;/a&gt; is about 5.9 km, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1032344746/el-rodenal-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Rodenal Trail&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1031411653/camino-del-cid-molina-de-aragon-and-castilnuevo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino del Cid: Molina de Aragón and Castilnuevo Loop&lt;/a&gt; are both around 12.7 km. Most moderate runners can complete these in 1 to 2 hours.

Q: What do other runners enjoy about the trails in Molina de Aragón?
A: Runners using komoot often praise the varied terrain and the opportunity to combine physical activity with exploring the region's natural and historical heritage. The trails offer a unique blend of river gorges, historic castles, and diverse landscapes within protected natural parks, making for a rewarding experience.

Q: Are there any specific trails that are particularly scenic?
A: Many trails offer scenic beauty, but the &lt;strong&gt;Barranco de la Hoz&lt;/strong&gt; is particularly renowned. While not a specific route in this guide, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1032344746/el-rodenal-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Rodenal Trail&lt;/a&gt; winds through the natural landscapes of the Alto Tajo Natural Park, offering beautiful views. Routes that pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5302621" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Molina de Aragón&lt;/a&gt; also provide picturesque backdrops.

Q: Is Molina de Aragón a good destination for combining running with other outdoor activities?
A: Absolutely. Molina de Aragón is an excellent base for outdoor enthusiasts. Beyond running, the region is well-equipped for hiking, cycling, bird watching, canoeing/kayaking in the Alto Tajo, climbing, rappelling, and even speleology and geocaching. The extensive network of signposted trails caters to a variety of activities, making it easy to combine your run with other adventures.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Molina de Aragón?
A: The region's diverse landscapes can be enjoyed throughout the year. Spring and autumn generally offer milder temperatures, making them ideal for running. Summer can be warm, so early morning or late afternoon runs are recommended. Winter running is also possible, with the landscape taking on a different, often serene, beauty, though you should be prepared for cooler temperatures.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by the Tower of Aragón?
A: Yes, the &lt;strong&gt;Tower of Aragón&lt;/strong&gt; is a prominent landmark in Molina de Aragón. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20143284" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Molina de Aragón – Tower of Aragón loop from Molina de Aragón&lt;/a&gt; is a 9.1 km route that specifically incorporates this historic tower, offering a great way to see it up close while enjoying your run.
tips: In the castle of Molina it is necessary to distinguish the fortress surrounded by walls, and the so-called Torre de Aragón. The fortress is from the 12th century and was built by Manrique de Lara, the first lord of Molina. The Torre de Aragón was built on the Arab castle, and this on a previous Celtiberian castro.
Spanish municipality in the province of Guadalajara through which the tourist route called Camino del Cid passes.

faqs.question: Is this loop suitable for beginner joggers?
faqs.answer: Yes, the loop is generally considered suitable for jogging, with a moderate difficulty rating. The 2 km "Castilnuevo-Molina de Aragón Multi Trail" section, for instance, has a moderate physical rating. While some parts of the broader region can be rugged, this specific loop, if it sticks to established paths and the doubletrack, should be manageable for those with some jogging experience.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Camino del Cid: Molina de Aragón and Castilnuevo Loop?
faqs.answer: The terrain varies, offering a mix of experiences. You'll jog through historical town paths in Molina de Aragón, including its Jewish and Muslim quarters, and encounter paved surfaces around its castle and churches. Between Molina de Aragón and Castilnuevo, there's a 2 km doubletrack multi-use trail. Sections along the Gallo River are pleasant, and as the route is within the Alto Tajo Natural Park, some parts may involve more natural, potentially uneven, surfaces.

faqs.question: What historical landmarks will I see on this route?
faqs.answer: This loop is rich in history. You'll pass by the impressive medieval Castle of Molina de Aragón, the old Romanesque bridge over the Gallo River, and ancient churches like Santa Clara and San Martín. In Castilnuevo, you can see the Castilnuevo Castle and its historic town center.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to visit for jogging would generally be during spring or autumn when temperatures are milder. These seasons offer pleasant weather for outdoor activities and allow you to enjoy both the historical sites and natural scenery without the extreme heat of summer or the cold of winter.

faqs.question: Does this loop connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several official trails. You'll find yourself on parts of the Camino del Cid (Senderista), GR 160 - Camino del Cid BTT, and Las Tres Taifas, among others.

faqs.question: Is the Camino del Cid: Molina de Aragón and Castilnuevo Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for this exact loop aren't detailed, generally, trails in natural parks like Alto Tajo allow dogs, often requiring them to be on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other users. It's always a good idea to check local signage upon arrival.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail or the Geoparque de la Comarca de Molina-Alto Tajo?
faqs.answer: There are no specific permits or entrance fees required to jog this loop or to access the general areas of the Geoparque de la Comarca de Molina-Alto Tajo. However, always respect local regulations and private property.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead for this loop?
faqs.answer: You can typically find parking within Molina de Aragón, especially near the historical center or the castle, which serves as a convenient starting point for the loop. Specific parking areas are usually well-marked within the town.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife might I encounter along the way?
faqs.answer: As the route is within the Alto Tajo Natural Park, you might spot diverse wildlife. Keep an eye out for deer, wildcats, and various bird species. The park's diverse ecosystems, including juniper, pine, and oak groves, provide habitats for many animals.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay near the trail?
faqs.answer: Molina de Aragón is a significant town and an essential stop on the Camino del Cid, so you'll find various hotels, restaurants, and cafes there. Castilnuevo, being smaller, will have fewer options but might offer local establishments. It's advisable to plan your meals and accommodation, especially if visiting during peak season.

faqs.question: What should I bring for jogging this loop?
faqs.answer: Given the varied terrain and moderate difficulty, comfortable jogging shoes suitable for both paved and natural surfaces are essential. Bring water, especially on warmer days, and consider light layers of clothing. A small backpack for essentials and a camera to capture the historical and natural beauty would also be useful.
